Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated creatinine, potassium, chloride, total bilirubin, hematocrit, aPTT, cholesterol, and LDL, with low arterial pH and oxygen saturation. These findings suggest impaired renal function, possible metabolic acidosis, mild hyperbilirubinemia, and dyslipidemia. Liver enzymes are not elevated, but coagulation is slightly prolonged. Recommend further evaluation of renal and hepatic function, acid-base status, and cardiovascular risk.
